active_conjecture.summary: For W_n=(n-2,2) and alpha_n=xi_n=(n-3,2,1), sparse multiplicity-four spectra through n=11 reconstruct monic integer quartics. Exact marked-cycle equality patterns now prove their trace is 4n^3-46n^2+149n-118 for every n>=7, and 17 relative orbit classes prove Tr(H^2). The next theorem targets are Tr(H^3), Tr(H^4), and quartic root separation.

execution_model: Research generation and experiments run interactively during active Codex sessions. A GitHub workflow validates pushed snapshots, and a scheduled backup can commit only validation-passing changes; neither mechanism performs autonomous quantum-algorithm research between sessions.

milestones.detail: Candidates now require reductions, complexity accounting, falsifiers, classical baselines, and proof obligations before acceptance.
milestones.title: Toy circuit search was removed

milestones.detail: Diagonal Jucys-Murphy labels are accessible, but exact multiplicity degeneracy proves that labels alone are not the internal Kronecker transform.
milestones.title: A nonabelian transform was split into precise subproblems

milestones.detail: Exact Specht polytabloids give raw gap 2(n-2) and LCU-normalized gap 2/[n(n-1)] for one stable multiplicity-two family.
milestones.title: A restricted all-n commutant gap was proved

milestones.detail: A second bounded-support Hamiltonian splits residual multiplicities up to four and produces unitary left/right matrices in all ten final sectors.
milestones.title: The complete finite S_6 Racah table was resolved

milestones.detail: Exact falling-cycle and relative-orbit calculations prove Tr(H), Tr(H^2), and hence the first two characteristic coefficients for every n>=7.
milestones.title: Two stable quartic coefficients were proved

next_actions.detail: Generalize the relative-orbit engine to H^3 and H^4; the first and second power traces are now proved.
next_actions.title: Evaluate the next two power traces

next_actions.detail: Derive traces H^2, H^3, and H^4, apply Newton identities, and prove inverse-polynomial root separation after LCU normalization.
next_actions.title: Recover the full stable quartic

next_actions.detail: Turn nested labels into a uniform coherent circuit and show that its outcomes recover a hidden involution beyond legal graph/code baselines.
next_actions.title: Compile and test the actual decoder

overview: The project is now useful primarily as a research filter. It has replaced tiny-circuit search with proof obligations, access-model accounting, classical attacks, exact finite representation theory, and a permanent negative-result memory. The strongest surviving lead is not an algorithm: it is a specific bounded-support operator hierarchy with exact and falsifiable spectral theorem targets in a nonabelian HSP route.

tracks.evidence: The all-target census evaluated millions of legal targets; the remaining question is a source-conditioned reduced-basis theorem, not another target sweep.
tracks.next: Prove a random-label LLL geometry law or abandon nearest-plane branching.
tracks.summary: Exact source models, sieve accounting, lattice attacks, and marker-aware decoders were implemented. Fixed-depth nearest-plane lists eventually collapse, so small-n decoder success is not evidence of a polynomial algorithm.
tracks.title: Hidden shift / DHSP

tracks.evidence: Current triage retains 0 viable code rows; the last Goppa syzygy collision was resolved by the hull-projector route.
tracks.next: Generate a new natural family that survives the complete classical triage gate.
tracks.summary: Goppa, quasi-cyclic, finite-geometry, rank-metric, and CFI-derived rows were attacked with support, hull, Schur, syzygy, projector, recovery, and graph-side invariants.
tracks.title: Code equivalence

tracks.evidence: YJM spectra were verified on 3 sectors, and bounded-support commutants split 2 finite multiplicity sectors up to multiplicity 5. The stable multiplicity-two family now has 1 exact all-n gap theorem; the hierarchy resolves 10/10 finite S_6 sectors. Sparse stable probes reconstruct 5 integer quartics through n=11.
tracks.next: Prove the stable quartic coefficients and root gaps exactly, then compile the hierarchy coherently.
tracks.summary: The project separated efficient Fourier/tableau labels from the genuinely open multiplicity-space, Racah, transition-filter, and hidden-involution decoder operations.
tracks.title: Nonabelian coset states

verdict.detail: Every speedup claim remains blocked. One restricted representation-theoretic gap theorem is now proved, a bounded-support hierarchy resolves the complete finite S_6 Racah table, and two stable quartic coefficients are exact. The remaining coefficients, root gaps, coherent recoupling, and decoding remain open.
verdict.title: No breakthrough yet
